retrieval_text: Tamarind is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 198 works and 936 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Tamarind, Tamarinds. Common plant-part co-mentions include Fruit, Flower, Gum, Root, Bark, Berry. Common preparation co-mentions include Decoction, Tea, Infusion, Juice, Oil, Powder. Common condition co-mentions include Fever, Inflammation, Vomiting or nausea, Cold or catarrh, Wounds or ulcers, Rheumatism. Caution co-mentions include Bleeding caution, Dose caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Fatal-risk warning, Toxicity watch, Child-use caution. Representative source works include Domestic medicine , or, A treatise on the prevention and cure of diseases, by regimen and simple medicines : with an appendix, containing a dispensatory for the use of private practioners; Domestic medicine , or, A treatise on the prevention and cure of diseases, by regimen and simple medicines : with an appendix, containing a dispensatory for the use of private practioners; Domestic medicine , or, A treatise on the prevention and cure of diseases, by regimen and simple medicines : with an appendix, containing a dispensatory for the use of private practioners; Domestic medicine , or, A treatise on the prevention and cure of diseases, by regimen and simple medicines : with an appendix, containing a dispensatory for the use of private practioners. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
